config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters a lot, but it should not automatically come before individual rights, because that can easily be used to justify abuse, censorship, and unfair treatment. From my perspective, a healthy society protects people while also respecting personal freedom and dignity, and the state should only limit rights when there is a clear and serious public need. In practice, I believe the best societies are those that balance the common good with strong protections for the individual.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kita penuh dengan contoh bagaimana alasan “demi masyarakat” bisa dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan kebebasan berbicara, diskriminasi, dan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi hak individu harus menjadi batas yang kuat, bukan sesuatu yang mudah disingkirkan setiap kali mayoritas merasa itu praktis. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak dasar tetap dilindungi, bahkan saat itu tidak nyaman bagi sebagian orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over individual rights. From my perspective as an Indonesian, I have seen how appeals to “the public interest” can be used to silence criticism, pressure minorities, or excuse unfair treatment, so I am very cautious about giving society a blank check. At the same time, I do not think individual rights are absolute in every case, because public health, safety, and order do matter, but any limit should be narrow, necessary, and accountable. A good society is not one that crushes the individual for the majority; it is one that protects people and still respects dignity, freedom, and justice.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena, dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai sebagai alasan untuk menekan hak yang seharusnya dilindungi. Pengalaman di Amerika menunjukkan bahwa mayoritas atau pemerintah bisa keliru, dan ketika itu terjadi, hak individu justru berfungsi sebagai pagar agar kekuasaan tidak semena-mena. Saya setuju bahwa kesehatan publik, keselamatan, dan ketertiban kadang memerlukan pembatasan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Jadi bagi saya, prioritas utama bukan masyarakat di atas individu, melainkan menjaga keseimbangan di mana hak dasar tetap menjadi batas yang kuat bagi apa pun yang disebut “demi kepentingan umum.”

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my Indonesian perspective, that idea can sound appealing in the name of harmony, but in practice it is often used to silence criticism, pressure minorities, and excuse abuse by authorities or the majority. Society does need order, public health, and shared responsibility, but those goals should never become a blank check to override dignity and basic freedoms. For me, the right balance is a society strong enough to protect the common good without treating individual rights as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu; menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat betapa sering alasan “demi masyarakat” dipakai untuk membenarkan sensor, diskriminasi, atau perluasan kuasa negara, padahal mayoritas pun bisa salah. Memang benar kesehatan publik, keselamatan, dan ketertiban kadang menuntut pembatasan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa diuji, bukan dijadikan dalih umum. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru ditandai oleh kemampuan melindungi kepentingan bersama tanpa mengorbankan hak dasar sebagai hal yang mudah dikalahkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
